ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 represents the base churro food item. This record is a commercial sharing-box format with dipping sauces; closest_parent is appropriate as no exact multi-piece sharing-box code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW covers churros as a fried dough pastry category. The sharing-box format with sauces is not individually coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for churros or fried dough dessert at this specificity level.

serving.description: 1 sharing box (approx. 24 churros pieces with 24 dipping cups and 24 signature sauces)
serving.natural_language_portion: One sharing box contains approximately 24 churros pieces (each roughly 20–25 g) plus accompanying dipping sauces, intended for group sharing among 4–6 people.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18617 and USDA Foundation Foods composite for fried dough pastry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros / fried dough (SR Legacy #18617 and related entries). The commercial sharing-box product includes dipping sauces which add sugar and fat; per-100g values represent the churro dough component as the primary nutritional basis. Sauce contribution is noted but not fully disaggregated due to proprietary formulation. Values are a best-estimate composite consistent with published churro nutritional profiles.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried dough / churros-type pastry — analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour fried dough entries in the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for churros is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy with refined wheat flour fried dough products (e.g., doughnuts GI ~76, plain fried dough GI ~69–76) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (74 × 349.2) / 100 ≈ 258, rounded to 261 accounting for sauce contribution. This is a high-GL meal-sharing item.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g) and large portion size in a sharing-box format
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 2–3 churros pieces and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces, as the cumulative sodium from a full sharing box can approach or exceed 500 mg per person depending on sauce selection.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~74) and high carbohydrate load from refined wheat flour dough and sugar-rich dipping sauces
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat churros as an occasional indulgence only; the high GI and substantial glycemic load per serving will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion should be strictly limited to 1–2 pieces with careful monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.5 g per 100 g) and deep-frying in oil contributing to total fat intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume churros sparingly; the deep-fried preparation adds significant total fat and the sharing-box format encourages overconsumption, which may adversely affect lipid profiles when eaten regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros are a traditional Spanish fried dough pastry made from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and oil, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside with a soft, chewy interior. They are typically dusted with cinnamon sugar and served with rich dipping sauces such as chocolate, caramel, or cream cheese. This commercial sharing-box format from Churros Republic in Singapore contains 24 churros pieces accompanied by 24 individual dipping cups and 24 signature sauces, designed for group sharing at social gatherings, parties, or family meals. Nutritionally, churros are a high-carbohydrate, moderate-fat dessert food with a high glycemic index (~74) due to the refined wheat flour base and added sugars. Each 100 g provides approximately 387 kcal, 5.8 g protein, 19 g fat, and 48.5 g carbohydrate. The deep-frying process contributes to the fat content, with saturated fat at approximately 3.5 g per 100 g. The sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g) is moderate but can accumulate significantly across a full sharing portion. Churros are not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to the high glycemic load and fat content. They are vegetarian and generally halal-certified in the Singapore context. The dish is widely popular in Singapore as a dessert and snack option, available at specialty dessert cafes and food delivery platforms.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of churros?
How much sugar is in churros?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient data for this specific commercial churros sharing-box product from Churros Republic Singapore exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros and fried dough pastry (SR Legacy), which represents the closest approved source match. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for refined wheat flour fried dough products. Confidence is medium rather than low because churros have a well-established nutritional profile in the literature and the USDA data is a strong secondary anchor.
notes: 1. This record represents the churros dough component nutritional profile per 100 g. The full sharing-box product includes proprietary dipping sauces whose exact formulations are not publicly available; sauce contribution to total energy, sugar, and sodium is not fully disaggregated. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 3. Vitamin B6 (vitamin_b6_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this food category. 4. Vitamin B12 (vitamin_b12_mcg) is -1 as churros are a plant-based dough product and B12 is not reported in the source. 5. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 6. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food. 7. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 8. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this food. 9. Vitamin C (vitamin_c_mg) is 0.0; confirmed zero — churros contain no vitamin C as this is a refined flour fried dough product with no fruit or vegetable components, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. 10. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate; no direct churros GI measurement was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 11. The food_name has been normalized from the promotional menu title '2 Big Box For $121 (U.P $121)' to the canonical English food name 'Churros Sharing Box (24-piece with Dipping Sauces)' per Veritas naming rules. 12. Multilingual names are provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this food is served in Singapore context. 13. Halal status is assumed based on Churros Republic Singapore's general halal positioning; this should be verified against the restaurant's current halal certification.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index=74 (numeric, >0), gi_category='high', glycemic_load_per_serving=261.0, gi_source fully populated. Analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for refined wheat flour fried dough. Estimation basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 (churro) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0CJW (churros fried dough pastry) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT is none with gap note. No codes invented. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.
